name: gef
title: gef

commands.name: gef

arguments.description: Arguments after executable-file are passed to inferior.
arguments.raw: --args             Arguments after executable-file are passed to inferior.

arguments.description: Analyze the core dump COREFILE.
Use EXECFILE as the executable.
Attach to running process PID.
Search for source files in DIR.
arguments.raw: --directory=DIR    Search for source files in DIR.

arguments.description: Use FILE as symbol file and executable file.
arguments.raw: --se=FILE          Use FILE as symbol file and executable file.

arguments.description: Read symbols from SYMFILE.
Fully read symbol files on first access.
arguments.raw: --readnow          Fully read symbol files on first access.

arguments.description: Do not read symbol files.
Set writing into executable and core files.
arguments.raw: --write            Set writing into executable and core files.

arguments.description: Do not read ~/.gdbinit.
Do not read any .gdbinit files in any directory.
arguments.raw: --nx               Do not read any .gdbinit files in any directory.

arguments.description: Output information used by emacs-GDB interface.
arguments.raw: --fullname         Output information used by emacs-GDB interface.

arguments.description: Use TTY for input/output by the program being debugged.
arguments.raw: --tty=TTY          Use TTY for input/output by the program being debugged.

arguments.description: Use the GUI interface.
Do not use the GUI interface.
arguments.raw: --nw               Do not use the GUI interface.

arguments.description: Use a terminal user interface.
Exit after processing options.
Like --batch, but suppress all gdb stdout output.
arguments.raw: --batch-silent     Like --batch, but suppress all gdb stdout output.

arguments.description: Print details about GDB configuration and then exit.
arguments.raw: --configuration    Print details about GDB configuration and then exit.

arguments.description: Print this message and then exit.
arguments.raw: --help             Print this message and then exit.

arguments.description: Print version information and then exit.
arguments.raw: --version          Print version information and then exit.

arguments.description: Set serial port baud rate used for remote debugging.
arguments.raw: -b BAUDRATE        Set serial port baud rate used for remote debugging.

arguments.description: Set timeout in seconds for remote debugging.
arguments.raw: -l TIMEOUT         Set timeout in seconds for remote debugging.

arguments.description: Change current directory to DIR.

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# gef -h
 This is the GNU debugger.  Usage:
 
   gdb [options] [executable-file [core-file or process-id]]
   gdb [options] --args executable-file [inferior-arguments ...]
 
 Selection of debuggee and its files:
 
 --args             Arguments after executable-file are passed to inferior.
 --core=COREFILE    Analyze the core dump COREFILE.
 --exec=EXECFILE    Use EXECFILE as the executable.
 --pid=PID          Attach to running process PID.
 --directory=DIR    Search for source files in DIR.
 --se=FILE          Use FILE as symbol file and executable file.
 --symbols=SYMFILE  Read symbols from SYMFILE.
 --readnow          Fully read symbol files on first access.
 --readnever        Do not read symbol files.
 --write            Set writing into executable and core files.
 
 Initial commands and command files:
 
 --command=FILE, -x Execute GDB commands from FILE.
 --init-command=FILE, -ix
 		     Like -x but execute commands before loading inferior.
 --eval-command=COMMAND, -ex
 		     Execute a single GDB command.
 		     May be used multiple times and in conjunction
 		     with --command.
 --init-eval-command=COMMAND, -iex
 		     Like -ex but before loading inferior.
 --nh               Do not read ~/.gdbinit.
 --nx               Do not read any .gdbinit files in any directory.
 
 Output and user interface control:
 
 --fullname         Output information used by emacs-GDB interface.
 --interpreter=INTERP
 		     Select a specific interpreter / user interface.
 --tty=TTY          Use TTY for input/output by the program being debugged.
 -w                 Use the GUI interface.
 --nw               Do not use the GUI interface.
 --tui              Use a terminal user interface.
 -q, --quiet, --silent
 		     Do not print version number on startup.
 
 Operating modes:
 
 --batch            Exit after processing options.
 --batch-silent     Like --batch, but suppress all gdb stdout output.
 --return-child-result
 		     GDB exit code will be the child's exit code.
 --configuration    Print details about GDB configuration and then exit.
 --help             Print this message and then exit.
 --version          Print version information and then exit.
 
 Remote debugging options:
 
 -b BAUDRATE        Set serial port baud rate used for remote debugging.
 -l TIMEOUT         Set timeout in seconds for remote debugging.
 
 Other options:
 
 --cd=DIR           Change current directory to DIR.
 --data-directory=DIR, -D
 		     Set GDB's data-directory to DIR.
 
 At startup, GDB reads the following early init files and executes their
 commands:
  None found.
 
 At startup, GDB reads the following init files and executes their commands:
  * system-wide init files: /etc/gdb/gdbinit
 
 For more information, type "help" from within GDB, or consult the
 GDB manual (available as on-line info or a printed manual).
 
 Report bugs to <https://www.gnu.org/software/gdb/bugs/>.
 
 You can ask GDB-related questions on the GDB users mailing list
 (gdb@sourceware.org) or on GDB's IRC channel (#gdb on Libera.Chat).

local_help.--help:
This is the GNU debugger.  Usage:

    gdb [options] [executable-file [core-file or process-id]]
    gdb [options] --args executable-file [inferior-arguments ...]

Selection of debuggee and its files:

  --args             Arguments after executable-file are passed to inferior.
  --core=COREFILE    Analyze the core dump COREFILE.
  --exec=EXECFILE    Use EXECFILE as the executable.
  --pid=PID          Attach to running process PID.
  --directory=DIR    Search for source files in DIR.
  --se=FILE          Use FILE as symbol file and executable file.
  --symbols=SYMFILE  Read symbols from SYMFILE.
  --readnow          Fully read symbol files on first access.
  --readnever        Do not read symbol files.
  --write            Set writing into executable and core files.

Initial commands and command files:

  --command=FILE, -x Execute GDB commands from FILE.
  --init-command=FILE, -ix
		     Like -x but execute commands before loading inferior.
  --eval-command=COMMAND, -ex
		     Execute a single GDB command.
		     May be used multiple times and in conjunction
		     with --command.
  --init-eval-command=COMMAND, -iex
		     Like -ex but before loading inferior.
  --nh               Do not read ~/.gdbinit.
  --nx               Do not read any .gdbinit files in any directory.

Output and user interface control:

  --fullname         Output information used by emacs-GDB interface.
  --interpreter=INTERP
		     Select a specific interpreter / user interface.
  --tty=TTY          Use TTY for input/output by the program being debugged.
  -w                 Use the GUI interface.
  --nw               Do not use the GUI interface.
  --tui              Use a terminal user interface.
  -q, --quiet, --silent
		     Do not print version number on startup.

Operating modes:

  --batch            Exit after processing options.
  --batch-silent     Like --batch, but suppress all gdb stdout output.
  --return-child-result
		     GDB exit code will be the child's exit code.
  --configuration    Print details about GDB configuration and then exit.
  --help             Print this message and then exit.
  --version          Print version information and then exit.

Remote debugging options:

  -b BAUDRATE        Set serial port baud rate used for remote debugging.
  -l TIMEOUT         Set timeout in seconds for remote debugging.

Other options:

  --cd=DIR           Change current directory to DIR.
  --data-directory=DIR, -D
		     Set GDB's data-directory to DIR.

At startup, GDB reads the following early init files and executes their
commands:
   None found.

At startup, GDB reads the following init files and executes their commands:
   * system-wide init files: /etc/gdb/gdbinit

For more information, type "help" from within GDB, or consult the
GDB manual (available as on-line info or a printed manual).

Report bugs to <https://www.gnu.org/software/gdb/bugs/>.

You can ask GDB-related questions on the GDB users mailing list
(gdb@sourceware.org) or on GDB's IRC channel (#gdb on Libera.Chat).

local_help.apropos:
proc_kpageflags (5)  - physical pages frame masks
lat_pagefault (8)    - measure the cost of pagefaulting pages from a file
TIFFMergeFieldInfo (3tiff) - add application-defined TIFF tags to the list of...

binary_metadata.file_type: POSIX shell script, ASCII text executable

apt_metadata.description:
modern experience for GDB with advanced debugging capabilities
GEF is a set of commands for x86/64, ARM, MIPS, PowerPC and SPARC to assist
exploit developers and reverse-engineers when using old school GDB. It
provides additional features to GDB using the Python API to assist during the
process of dynamic analysis and exploit development. Application developers
will also benefit from it, as GEF lifts a great part of regular GDB obscurity,
avoiding repeating traditional commands, or bringing out the relevant
information from the debugging runtime.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use gef because ### gef.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair gef with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `gef --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ### gef.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install gef
Install with: sudo apt install gef
